links.targetTitle: Recovery from Swarm walks the user manifest to discover drives, then replays operation batches with preserved original IDs.
The AES-256 sync key is deterministically derived from a MetaMask signature so any device rehydrates the same key

global.title: Recovery rehydrates Connect from wallet-only using user → drive → document manifest traversal
global.content:
When a user opens Connect on a new device or after clearing browser data, recovery proceeds from wallet-only state:

1. User connects MetaMask.
2. Plugin requests `personal_sign`, which re-derives the SAME Swarm key as before.
3. `SwarmChannel.pollInbox()` reads the user manifest from feed `ph:v2:user:<address>`.
4. For each drive listed, read the drive manifest at `ph:v2:drive:<driveId>`.
5. For each document not present locally: read its manifest at `ph:v2:doc:<docId>`, download each operation batch from `/bytes` (auto-decrypted), create SyncOperations, and enqueue in the inbox.
6. SyncManager applies via `reactor.load()` using the ORIGINAL document IDs, so links and cross-doc references survive.
7. The cursor advances in `sync_cursors`, the drive materializes in PGlite with correct folder structure, and future polls are incremental.

Key properties: cursor-based so nothing re-downloads; no race conditions because SyncManager processes inbox before outbox, preventing re-upload of just-recovered ops; failed operations flow to `sync_dead_letters` for retry.
global.description: MetaMask personal_sign rederives the same AES key; pollInbox reads user manifest, then drive manifests, then each doc manifest, downloads batches, and applies with original IDs via reactor.load().
